name: Gentle Mobility Flow
description: Light mobility work to reduce tension and support tissue recovery.

exercises.instructions: Roll each major muscle group slowly, pausing 20-30s on tight spots.
exercises.common_mistakes: Rolling too fast without pausing on tender areas.

exercises.instructions: Stand with hands on hips, draw large circles with your pelvis in both directions.
exercises.common_mistakes: Limiting range of motion — aim for the largest circles you can comfortably make.

exercises.instructions: On all fours, alternate between arching your back up (cat) and dropping your belly (cow) with your breath.
exercises.common_mistakes: Rushing the movement — let each rep be guided by a full inhale and exhale.

exercises.instructions: Kneel and stretch arms forward on the floor, sinking your hips back toward your heels.
exercises.common_mistakes: Holding tension in the shoulders — let them fall heavy toward the ground.

exercises.instructions: Sit tall, cross one leg over, place opposite elbow on knee, and rotate gently toward the back leg.
exercises.common_mistakes: Collapsing the spine — keep the torso tall before rotating.

name: Restorative Yoga
description: Parasympathetic-activating yoga sequence for high-stress or sleep-deprived days.

exercises.instructions: Lie on your back with legs resting vertically against a wall, arms relaxed by your sides.
exercises.common_mistakes: Placing the hips too far from the wall — scoot your sit bones as close to the wall as comfortable.

exercises.instructions: Lying on your back, draw one knee across the body and let it fall to the opposite side while extending the same-side arm out.
exercises.common_mistakes: Lifting the opposite shoulder — keep both shoulders grounded throughout.

exercises.instructions: Lie on your back, feet flat, lift hips and place a block or folded blanket under the sacrum.
exercises.common_mistakes: Placing the block too high — support should feel passive, not strained.

exercises.instructions: Lie flat, place one hand on chest and one on belly, breathe so only the belly hand rises.
exercises.common_mistakes: Breathing too shallowly into the chest — the belly should expand first on each inhale.

exercises.instructions: Lie completely flat, feet slightly apart, arms by your sides palms up, and let your body fully relax.
exercises.common_mistakes: Staying mentally active — use a body scan to consciously release each muscle group.

name: Active Recovery Walk & Breathwork
description: Low-intensity movement with nervous system regulation for elite athletes on recovery days.

exercises.instructions: Walk at a pace where you can hold a conversation but still feel slightly warm.
exercises.common_mistakes: Walking too fast and turning it into cardio — keep your heart rate conversational.

exercises.instructions: Inhale 4 counts, hold 4, exhale 4, hold 4 — repeat for full nervous system regulation.
exercises.common_mistakes: Straining to hold the breath — the counts should feel comfortable, not forced.

exercises.instructions: Kneel in a lunge, push hips forward and tuck the pelvis gently to feel a deep front-hip stretch.
exercises.common_mistakes: Arching the lower back — tuck your pelvis to deepen the stretch without lumbar strain.

exercises.instructions: Sit in a chair or on the floor, hands behind your head, rotate your upper back without moving your hips.
exercises.common_mistakes: Rotating from the lower back — the movement should come from the mid-spine only.

exercises.instructions: Grip a pull-up bar, let your body hang completely relaxed to decompress the spine and stretch the lats.
exercises.common_mistakes: Engaging the shoulders — let them rise toward the ears for full passive traction.

name: Full Body Circuit
description: Balanced full-body circuit suitable for regular training days.

exercises.instructions: Feet shoulder-width apart, lower until thighs are parallel to the ground, drive through heels to stand.
exercises.common_mistakes: Knees caving inward — push them out in line with your toes throughout.

exercises.instructions: Hands slightly wider than shoulders, lower chest to floor keeping elbows at 45°, press back up.
exercises.common_mistakes: Sagging hips — keep your core braced so your body forms a straight line.

exercises.instructions: Step one foot back, lower the rear knee toward the floor, then drive off the front foot to return.
exercises.common_mistakes: Letting the front knee travel past the toes — keep it stacked over the ankle.

exercises.instructions: Hinge forward, brace the core, pull the dumbbell to your hip keeping the elbow close to the body.
exercises.common_mistakes: Rotating the torso to lift heavier — keep hips square and use only your back and arm.

exercises.instructions: Forearms on the floor, maintain a straight line from head to heels, breathing steadily.
exercises.common_mistakes: Raising the hips too high or letting them sag — aim for a perfectly flat back.

exercises.instructions: In a push-up position, drive alternating knees toward your chest as fast as you can with control.
exercises.common_mistakes: Bouncing the hips up and down — keep them level and stable throughout.

name: Push / Pull Split
description: Classic push/pull split for consistent muscle stimulus on standard training days.

exercises.instructions: Grip just outside shoulder-width, lower bar to mid-chest with control, press back to lockout.
exercises.common_mistakes: Flaring elbows to 90° — keep them at roughly 45-75° to protect the shoulder joint.

exercises.instructions: Hinge at the hips to roughly 45°, pull bar to lower chest leading with elbows, squeeze the back at the top.
exercises.common_mistakes: Using momentum by jerking upright — keep the torso angle fixed throughout the set.

exercises.instructions: Stand with bar at collar-bone height, press directly overhead to full lockout, lower with control.
exercises.common_mistakes: Hyperextending the lower back — brace your core and keep a neutral spine.

exercises.instructions: Dead hang, depress and retract the scapulae, pull chin over the bar leading with your elbows.
exercises.common_mistakes: Kipping or swinging — use strict form to maximize lat activation.

exercises.instructions: Slight bend in the elbows, raise dumbbells to shoulder height leading with the pinkies, lower slowly.
exercises.common_mistakes: Shrugging the traps — keep shoulders depressed and raise with the delts only.

exercises.instructions: Set the cable at face height, pull rope toward your nose with elbows high, externally rotating at the top.
exercises.common_mistakes: Pulling too heavy — the rear delts are small; use a weight that allows full rotation.

exercises.instructions: Grip parallel bars, lower until upper arms are parallel to the floor, press back to full lockout.
exercises.common_mistakes: Leaning too far forward — slight forward lean is fine, but avoid collapsing at the chest.

name: Upper / Lower Split
description: High-volume upper/lower session for advanced trainees maintaining consistent progress.

exercises.instructions: Hip-width stance, bar over mid-foot, neutral spine, drive through the floor and lock out hips and knees simultaneously.
exercises.common_mistakes: Rounding the lower back — brace the core hard and maintain a long spine before the bar leaves the floor.

exercises.instructions: Attach a plate via a belt, use a full dead hang to start, pull chin cleanly over the bar with control.
exercises.common_mistakes: Cutting the range of motion short — go to a full dead hang on every rep.

exercises.instructions: Controlled hinge, pull bar to the lower abdomen with a tight upper back, pause briefly at the top.
exercises.common_mistakes: Jerking the weight — the eccentric (lowering) phase should take at least 2 seconds.

exercises.instructions: Feet shoulder-width on the platform, lower until knees reach 90°, press evenly through both feet.
exercises.common_mistakes: Allowing the lower back to peel off the pad — stop the descent before that happens.

exercises.instructions: Hinge from the hips with a soft knee bend, lower bar along the thighs until you feel a hamstring stretch, then drive hips forward.
exercises.common_mistakes: Bending the knees too much — this becomes a squat rather than a hip hinge.

exercises.instructions: Sit tall, pull the handle to the lower abdomen, squeeze the shoulder blades together, then fully extend with control.
exercises.common_mistakes: Leaning back to initiate the pull — use only the arms and back, keeping the torso stable.

exercises.instructions: Lie prone on the machine, curl heels toward your glutes through a full range of motion, lower slowly.
exercises.common_mistakes: Lifting the hips off the pad — keep them pressed down to isolate the hamstrings.

name: Power Activation Circuit
description: Plyometric activation session to maximize output on peak energy days.

exercises.instructions: Stand in front of the box, swing arms, jump with both feet, land softly with bent knees, step down carefully.
exercises.common_mistakes: Landing with straight legs — always absorb the impact with a deep knee bend.

exercises.instructions: Lower to the floor, then press explosively enough for your hands to leave the ground briefly.
exercises.common_mistakes: Not maintaining a rigid body position — a sagging core bleeds power.

exercises.instructions: Descend to a squat, then explode upward as high as possible, land softly and absorb into the next rep.
exercises.common_mistakes: Landing with a stiff impact — let your ankles, knees, and hips all absorb the landing.

exercises.instructions: Sprint at maximum effort for 20m, walk back to start for recovery, then repeat.
exercises.common_mistakes: Not achieving true maximum effort — each sprint should feel all-out.

exercises.instructions: Raise the ball overhead, engage the core, and slam it into the ground as hard as possible, catching the bounce.
exercises.common_mistakes: Slamming without a full overhead extension — start each rep with the ball fully raised.

exercises.instructions: Alternate arm waves as fast as possible while keeping a stable athletic stance throughout.
exercises.common_mistakes: Losing core stability — keep the torso tall and avoid swaying side to side.

name: Heavy Compound Day
description: Max-strength compound session reserved for high-performance windows.

exercises.instructions: Bar on upper traps, feet shoulder-width, descend until hip crease passes the knee, drive through the whole foot.
exercises.common_mistakes: Caving knees on the ascent — cue yourself to push knees out against your elbows as you drive up.

exercises.instructions: Arch the upper back, feet flat, lower bar to the lower chest and drive through an even bar path.
exercises.common_mistakes: Losing the upper back arch at the bottom — keep the scapulae retracted throughout.

exercises.instructions: Full dead hang to chin over bar, control the descent to maximize tension in the lats.
exercises.common_mistakes: Using a partial range of motion to handle more weight — full range builds far more strength.

exercises.instructions: From a rack at collar-bone height, brace hard, press to full lockout and shrug the traps at the top.
exercises.common_mistakes: Pressing in front of the head instead of straight up — the bar path should go directly overhead.

exercises.instructions: Pull bar from the floor, triple extend (ankle, knee, hip), shrug and pull under the bar to catch in a partial squat.
exercises.common_mistakes: Pulling with the arms too early — the legs and hips provide the power; arms just guide.

exercises.instructions: Carry heavy dumbbells or kettlebells at your sides, walk briskly with tall posture and tight core.
exercises.common_mistakes: Leaning to one side — use equal weight in both hands and keep the shoulders level.

name: Athletic Peak Performance
description: Elite-level functional session for peak performance windows — full CNS activation.

exercises.instructions: Pull bar from floor, receive in a front squat, stand, then split jerk overhead to full lockout.
exercises.common_mistakes: Failing to get under the bar quickly enough — the pull is just to create vertical momentum.

exercises.instructions: Working at 85% of your 1RM, treat each set as a near-maximal effort with perfect form.
exercises.common_mistakes: Grinding through technical breakdown — if form collapses, reduce the load.

exercises.instructions: Attach a heavy plate, perform full-range dips with controlled descent and explosive press.
exercises.common_mistakes: Partial range of motion — the upper arm must reach parallel for a full rep.

exercises.instructions: From a dead hang, generate a powerful pull and transition your chest above the bar into a dip lockout.
exercises.common_mistakes: Relying on a kip without developing the straight-bar strength — build strict pull strength first.

exercises.instructions: Load the sled heavy, lean forward at 45°, drive with short powerful steps through the forefoot.
exercises.common_mistakes: Standing too upright — a forward lean transfers your force into horizontal movement.

exercises.instructions: Use competition-level weights, walk as fast as possible while maintaining perfect posture.
exercises.common_mistakes: Taking wide, waddling steps — keep feet close to the midline for efficiency.

exercises.instructions: Go all-out for 20 seconds pushing and pulling the handles and pedaling simultaneously.
exercises.common_mistakes: Pacing yourself — true sprints should leave you unable to speak at the end.

name: Cycle Yoga & Restoration
description: Gentle cycle-aware yoga sequence to support the body during menstruation or high-fatigue phases.

exercises.instructions: Place a bolster or folded blanket under your torso, kneel and drape your body over it for full passive rest.
exercises.common_mistakes: Tensing the arms or hands — let every part of the body go completely heavy.

exercises.instructions: Lie on your back, bring the soles of the feet together, let knees fall open, arms relaxed by your sides.
exercises.common_mistakes: Forcing the knees toward the floor — let gravity do the work over time.

exercises.instructions: Lying on your back, draw both knees to chest and circle them slowly in both directions.
exercises.common_mistakes: Making the circles too large and straining the lower back — keep the movement small and controlled.

exercises.instructions: Draw one knee to the chest with both hands, keep the opposite leg extended flat on the floor.
exercises.common_mistakes: Lifting the head and neck — keep the head grounded for full lower-back release.

exercises.instructions: Scoot your hips to the wall, extend legs up, and let your body melt into the floor completely.
exercises.common_mistakes: Having the hips too far from the wall — closer is better for venous return.

exercises.instructions: Lying flat, mentally travel from your feet to your head, consciously releasing tension in each area.
exercises.common_mistakes: Rushing through areas that feel fine — spend extra time on any part that holds tension.

name: Light Strength — Follicular Phase
description: Progressive light-strength session to build momentum as follicular energy begins to rise.

exercises.instructions: Hold a dumbbell at chest height, feet shoulder-width, squat deep keeping your elbows inside your knees.
exercises.common_mistakes: Letting the chest fall forward — the dumbbell acts as a counterbalance, so use it.

exercises.instructions: Lie on a bench, lower dumbbells to chest level with elbows at 45°, press back to full extension.
exercises.common_mistakes: Touching the dumbbells at the top — keep slight tension in the chest throughout.

exercises.instructions: Sit tall at a low-cable, pull the handle to your lower abdomen, squeeze the shoulder blades at the end.
exercises.common_mistakes: Leaning back excessively — use a slight backward lean but drive the movement from the back.

exercises.instructions: Raise light dumbbells to shoulder height with arms almost straight, lower with a 3-second count.
exercises.common_mistakes: Using too much weight and turning it into a shrug — light weight with a slow negative is most effective.

exercises.instructions: Lie on your back, feet flat, drive hips toward the ceiling squeezing the glutes hard at the top.
exercises.common_mistakes: Hyperextending the lower back — squeeze the glutes, don't push through the lower back.

exercises.instructions: Stand side-on to a cable, hold the handle at chest, press it straight out and resist rotation, then return.
exercises.common_mistakes: Rotating the torso toward the cable — the entire goal is to resist that rotation.

name: Moderate Strength — Luteal Phase
description: Consistent moderate-intensity strength work that respects the elevated recovery demands of the luteal phase.

exercises.instructions: Push hips back, lower the bar along your thighs to mid-shin, keeping a long neutral spine throughout.
exercises.common_mistakes: Bending the knees too much and losing the hip-hinge pattern — keep a soft, fixed knee bend.

exercises.instructions: Moderate weight, full range of motion, focus on the mind-muscle connection over heavy loading.
exercises.common_mistakes: Rushing the reps — a deliberate tempo will produce better stimulus with less CNS demand.

exercises.instructions: Lean back slightly, pull the bar to the upper chest leading with elbows, control fully on the way up.
exercises.common_mistakes: Pulling behind the neck — always pull to the front to protect the cervical spine.

exercises.instructions: Take a long step forward, lower the back knee to just above the floor, then bring the rear foot forward to step again.
exercises.common_mistakes: Short stride length causing the front knee to travel too far over the toe.

exercises.instructions: Neutral-grip handle, pull to the navel with elbows close to the body, return with full arm extension.
exercises.common_mistakes: Letting the shoulders round forward at full extension — keep the scapulae slightly set.

exercises.instructions: In a forearm plank, slowly rotate hips to dip them toward each side alternately for oblique activation.
exercises.common_mistakes: Moving too fast and using momentum — each dip should be slow and controlled.

name: Gentle Walk & Restore
description: A short restorative walk with gentle stretching — ideal for menstruation or any day requiring minimal loading.

exercises.instructions: Walk at a comfortable, unhurried pace in fresh air if possible, focusing on relaxed breathing.
exercises.common_mistakes: Checking your phone — use this time to breathe and decompress mentally.

exercises.instructions: Step one foot back into a lunge, tuck the pelvis gently, and feel the front of the rear hip open.
exercises.common_mistakes: Arching the lower back — a slight posterior pelvic tilt deepens the stretch safely.

exercises.instructions: Hold a wall for balance, swing one leg side to side in a relaxed, pendulum motion to mobilize the hip.
exercises.common_mistakes: Swinging too hard — the movement should be passive and controlled, not forced.

exercises.instructions: Rise onto the balls of both feet, pause briefly at the top, lower slowly for full range of motion.
exercises.common_mistakes: Bouncing at the bottom — pause briefly to eliminate elastic energy and increase time under tension.

exercises.instructions: Roll both shoulders slowly backward in large circles, then reverse direction to release upper back tension.
exercises.common_mistakes: Hunching forward — stand tall so the shoulders can roll through a full range.
